beats.narration: Magnetic particle testing hangs on one trick: flux hates air. Force it through cracked steel, and the field betrays the flaw.

payload.title: FLUX LEAKAGE
payload.sub: How a magnetic field gives away the crack in its path.

beats.narration: First, the ground rules. Flux lines form closed loops, never cross, parallel lines repel, and flux always hunts the path of least reluctance.

payload.heading: Four ground rules of magnetic flux

beats.narration: Now add a crack. Some flux squeezes beneath it, some jumps the gap, and some escapes the surface: a leakage field that traps particles.

payload.heading: Flux meets a crack — three responses
payload.note: PARTICLES BRIDGE THE LEAK · CRACK MADE VISIBLE

beats.narration: Angle decides everything: leakage is strongest at ninety degrees, usable to about forty five, and a parallel crack hides. Every part gets two perpendicular shots.
Lock it in: flux only finds the crack it crosses. One shot is never enough. Magnetize twice, roughly ninety degrees apart.

payload.sub: Magnetize twice, roughly ninety degrees apart.
